seoTitle: Configure MX, SPF, and DKIM in Cloudflare (2026)
metaDescription: Set up mail routing and email authentication in Cloudflare by adding MX records, an SPF TXT rule, and DKIM keys to stop spoofing and delivery issues.
intro: Getting email working reliably on a domain means more than just pointing MX records at your mail host — you also need SPF and DKIM to prove your mail is legitimate. This guide covers adding all three record types in Cloudflare's DNS panel.

steps.heading: Open your DNS Records panel
steps.body: Select your domain zone in Cloudflare and navigate to DNS Records in the left sidebar.

steps.heading: Add your MX record
steps.body: Click Add Record, switch the type to MX, enter your root symbol, your mail server host address, and set the priority value.

steps.heading: Add an SPF TXT record
steps.body: Create a new TXT row containing your v=spf1 authorization string to declare which infrastructure is allowed to send mail on your behalf.

steps.heading: Add your DKIM record
steps.body: Create your mail selector DKIM record and paste in the public cryptographic key provided by your mail service.

steps.heading: Keep mail records DNS-only
steps.body: Verify that all MX, SPF, and DKIM records are set to 'DNS Only' (gray cloud) rather than proxied, since email traffic should bypass Cloudflare's proxy.

faq.question: Why do my mail records need to stay on 'DNS Only' instead of proxied?
faq.answer: Cloudflare's proxy is built for web traffic; mail-related records like MX, SPF, and DKIM need to resolve directly (DNS Only) so mail servers can read them correctly.

faq.question: What happens if I skip SPF or DKIM?
faq.answer: Without SPF and DKIM, your outgoing email is more likely to be flagged as spam or rejected outright by receiving mail servers that check for sender authentication.

faq.question: Can I have more than one MX record?
faq.answer: Yes, many mail setups use multiple MX records with different priority values as backups in case the primary mail server is unavailable.

faq.question: Where do I get the DKIM key values to paste into Cloudflare?
faq.answer: Your email service provider (such as Google Workspace or Microsoft 365) generates the DKIM selector and public key, which you then copy into a TXT record in Cloudflare.
